theme: The 10-week timeline: How to plan a perfect corporate trip

linkedin.text:
The 10-week corporate event timeline. The one we actually use.

Week 10-9: Discovery
Define objectives, budget range, group profile. Sign the engagement. Choose destination. Lock preliminary dates with hotel.

Week 8-7: Design
Program architecture: what is the experience narrative? Which activities deliver the business objective? First venue proposals. Flight options confirmed.

Week 6-5: Vendor contracts
Hotel contract signed. Key activities booked. Restaurant reservations locked. Local guide confirmed. All backups identified.

Week 4-3: Participant preparation
Invitations sent. Passport collection started. Dietary requirements surveyed. Room allocation planned. Pre-event communication sent.

Week 2: Final checks
All bookings confirmed. Transfers planned. Run-of-show document created. Team briefed. Client briefed. Contingency protocols reviewed.

Week 1: Pre-deployment
Final participant list locked. Packing guide sent. WhatsApp group created. On-site team flight confirmed.

Day 0: Go time
Airport meet-and-greet activated. Real-time operations center open. Client contact updated every 2 hours.

This timeline works for groups of 20-200. Compress it and things start breaking down.

The most expensive part of a bad event is the one you'll never see on an invoice: the trust you didn't build.

Planning something? Let's start 10 weeks before you need to.

📩 alon@upe.co.il | 🌐 upe.co.il

instagram.text:
The 10-week event planning timeline 📅

Week 10-9: Discovery (objectives, budget, destination)
Week 8-7: Program design + venue proposals
Week 6-5: Vendor contracts + activity bookings
Week 4-3: Invites + passport collection + rooms
Week 2: Final checks + run-of-show
Week 1: Pre-deployment + team brief
Day 0: Go time 🚀

Compress this and things break.

Save this for your next event planning conversation 👆

#EventPlanning #CorporateEvents #IncentiveTravel #Timeline #UproductionEvents #MICE #ProjectManagement
